Gastritis.message: These drugs may worsen gastric irritation and increase the risk of gastric ulcers.

Asthma.message: These drugs may trigger bronchospasm and worsen asthma symptoms.

Diabetes.message: These drugs can increase blood glucose levels and may require dose adjustments for diabetes medications.

Kidney Disease.message: These drugs can worsen kidney function and may require dose adjustments.

Hypertension.message: These drugs can increase blood pressure and may interfere with antihypertensive medications.

Peptic Ulcer.message: These drugs can increase the risk of bleeding and ulcer complications.

Liver Disease.message: These drugs can cause liver toxicity, especially in patients with existing liver disease.

Pregnancy.message: These drugs may pose risks during pregnancy and require careful consideration.

Bleeding Disorder.message: These drugs can increase the risk of bleeding complications.

Heart Failure.message: These drugs may worsen heart failure symptoms and require careful monitoring.

Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease.message: These drugs can cause respiratory depression and worsen breathing difficulties.

Glaucoma.message: These drugs can increase intraocular pressure and worsen glaucoma.

Seizure Disorder.message: These drugs can lower seizure threshold and increase seizure risk.

Gout.message: These drugs can increase uric acid levels and trigger gout attacks.

Thyroid Disease.message: These drugs can affect thyroid function and may require monitoring.

Osteoporosis.message: These drugs can decrease bone density and increase fracture risk.

Myasthenia Gravis.message: These drugs can worsen muscle weakness and precipitate myasthenic crisis.

Prostate Enlargement.message: These drugs can cause urinary retention and worsen obstructive symptoms.

Depression.message: These drugs may worsen depressive symptoms and require monitoring.

Anxiety Disorder.message: These drugs can increase anxiety symptoms and trigger panic attacks.

Arrhythmia.message: These drugs can prolong QT interval and increase arrhythmia risk.

Coronary Artery Disease.message: These drugs can increase cardiac workload and risk of ischemia.

Stroke History.message: These drugs require careful monitoring to balance bleeding and clotting risk.

Alzheimer Disease.message: These drugs can worsen cognitive impairment and increase fall risk.

Parkinson Disease.message: These drugs can worsen Parkinsonian symptoms and motor control.

Migraine.message: These drugs may trigger or worsen migraine headaches.

Inflammatory Bowel Disease.message: These drugs can trigger flares and worsen intestinal inflammation.

Celiac Disease.message: These medications may contain gluten and trigger symptoms.

Lactose Intolerance.message: These medications may contain lactose and cause digestive symptoms.

Peanut Allergy.message: These medications may contain peanut products and trigger allergic reactions.

Sulfa Allergy.message: These drugs may cause cross-reactivity in sulfa-allergic patients.

Penicillin Allergy.message: These drugs may cause cross-reactivity in penicillin-allergic patients.

G6PD Deficiency.message: These drugs can trigger hemolytic anemia in G6PD deficient patients.

Porphyria.message: These drugs can precipitate acute porphyria attacks.

Long QT Syndrome.message: These drugs can further prolong QT interval and increase arrhythmia risk.

Malignant Hyperthermia.message: These drugs can trigger life-threatening hyperthermia reaction.

Systemic Lupus Erythematosus.message: These drugs can induce or worsen lupus symptoms.

Rheumatoid Arthritis.message: These drugs require monitoring for infections and adverse effects.

Multiple Sclerosis.message: These drugs require careful monitoring of immune function and side effects.

HIV/AIDS.message: These drugs have multiple drug interactions and require careful management.

Hepatitis B.message: These drugs can cause hepatitis B reactivation and require monitoring.

Hepatitis C.message: These drugs can worsen liver function in hepatitis C patients.

Tuberculosis.message: These drugs can cause TB reactivation and require screening.

Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3.message: These drugs require dose adjustment or avoidance in moderate kidney disease.

Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 4.message: These drugs are contraindicated or require significant dose reduction in advanced kidney disease.

Dialysis.message: These drugs require special dosing considerations in dialysis patients.

Cirrhosis.message: These drugs can worsen hepatic encephalopathy and liver function.

Alcohol Use Disorder.message: These drugs have increased toxicity risk or abuse potential in alcohol users.

Substance Abuse History.message: These controlled substances require careful monitoring due to abuse potential.

Bipolar Disorder.message: These drugs can trigger manic episodes and require mood stabilizer coverage.

Schizophrenia.message: These drugs require monitoring for metabolic effects and drug interactions.

Eating Disorder.message: These drugs can increase seizure risk and worsen eating disorder symptoms.

Sleep Apnea.message: These drugs can worsen respiratory depression during sleep.

Obesity.message: These drugs can promote weight gain and worsen metabolic complications.

Anorexia.message: These drugs can worsen nutritional status and electrolyte imbalances.

Hyperlipidemia.message: These drugs can worsen lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk.

Hyperthyroidism.message: These drugs can worsen thyrotoxicosis and cardiac symptoms.

Hypothyroidism.message: These drugs can worsen thyroid function and may require dose adjustment.

Addison Disease.message: These situations can precipitate adrenal crisis in adrenal insufficiency.

Cushing Syndrome.message: Additional steroids can worsen metabolic and cardiovascular complications.

Acromegaly.message: These drugs can worsen symptoms and complications of excess growth hormone.

Hyperparathyroidism.message: These drugs can worsen hypercalcemia and related complications.

Hypoparathyroidism.message: These drugs can worsen hypocalcemia and require monitoring.

Pheochromocytoma.message: These drugs can precipitate hypertensive crisis in pheochromocytoma.

Carcinoid Syndrome.message: These drugs can trigger carcinoid crisis and worsen symptoms.

Mastocytosis.message: These drugs can trigger mast cell degranulation and anaphylaxis.

Sickle Cell Disease.message: These drugs can precipitate sickle cell crisis and vaso-occlusion.

Hemophilia.message: These drugs significantly increase bleeding risk in hemophilia patients.

Thrombophilia.message: These drugs increase thrombotic risk in hypercoagulable states.

Antiphospholipid Syndrome.message: These drugs significantly increase thrombotic risk in APS patients.

Polycythemia.message: These drugs can worsen blood viscosity and thrombotic risk.

Anemia.message: These drugs can worsen anemia through bleeding or other mechanisms.

Thrombocytopenia.message: These drugs significantly increase bleeding risk with low platelets.

Neutropenia.message: These drugs increase infection risk in patients with low white blood cells.

Bone Marrow Suppression.message: These drugs can worsen cytopenias and increase infection risk.

Post-Transplant.message: These require careful monitoring to maintain immunosuppression and prevent rejection.

Immunodeficiency.message: These can cause severe infections in immunocompromised patients.

Splenectomy.message: These patients require special vaccination protocols and infection prophylaxis.

Cystic Fibrosis.message: These drugs can worsen respiratory function in CF patients.

Bronchiectasis.message: These drugs increase infection risk and worsen respiratory function.

Interstitial Lung Disease.message: These drugs can cause or worsen pulmonary fibrosis.

Pulmonary Hypertension.message: These drugs can worsen pulmonary pressures and right heart function.

Pulmonary Embolism History.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent thromboembolism.

Deep Vein Thrombosis History.message: These increase risk of recurrent venous thromboembolism.

Atrial Fibrillation.message: These drugs can worsen arrhythmia control and increase stroke risk.

Bradycardia.message: These drugs can cause severe bradycardia and heart block.

Tachycardia.message: These drugs can worsen tachycardia and increase cardiac workload.

Aortic Stenosis.message: These drugs can cause severe hypotension in aortic stenosis.

Mitral Stenosis.message: These drugs reduce diastolic filling time and worsen symptoms.

Cardiomyopathy.message: These drugs can worsen cardiac function and heart failure.

Myocarditis.message: These drugs can worsen myocardial inflammation and function.

Pericarditis.message: These drugs increase risk of hemopericardium and cardiac tamponade.

Endocarditis.message: These drugs can worsen infection control and increase complications.

Peripheral Arterial Disease.message: These drugs can worsen peripheral perfusion and claudication.

Raynaud Phenomenon.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen vasospastic episodes.

Varicose Veins.message: These drugs can worsen venous insufficiency and thrombosis risk.

Lymphedema.message: These drugs can worsen fluid retention and edema.

Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease.message: These drugs can worsen reflux symptoms and esophageal damage.

Barrett Esophagus.message: These drugs can worsen esophageal inflammation and cancer risk.

Esophageal Varices.message: These drugs significantly increase risk of life-threatening variceal bleeding.

Hiatal Hernia.message: These drugs can worsen reflux and esophageal complications.

Dysphagia.message: These medications may be difficult to swallow or cause esophageal injury.

Achalasia.message: These drugs can worsen esophageal motility and dysphagia.

Dumping Syndrome.message: These can trigger dumping symptoms after gastric surgery.

Short Bowel Syndrome.message: These may not be absorbed properly in short bowel syndrome.

Malabsorption Syndrome.message: These may not be absorbed effectively and require alternative formulations.

Gastroparesis.message: These drugs can worsen gastric emptying and digestive symptoms.

Irritable Bowel Syndrome.message: These drugs can worsen bowel symptoms and motility.

Diverticulitis.message: These drugs may increase complication risk and mask symptoms.

Diverticulosis.message: These drugs may increase risk of diverticular bleeding and perforation.

Colitis.message: These drugs can trigger flares and worsen intestinal inflammation.

Constipation.message: These drugs can worsen constipation and require prophylactic laxatives.

Diarrhea.message: These drugs can worsen diarrhea and electrolyte imbalances.

Hemorrhoids.message: These drugs increase risk of rectal bleeding.

Anal Fissure.message: These drugs can worsen pain and delay healing.

Cholecystitis.message: These drugs can worsen biliary spasm and mask symptoms.

Gallstones.message: These drugs can promote gallstone formation or complications.

Pancreatitis.message: These drugs can cause or worsen pancreatitis.

Chronic Pancreatitis.message: These can worsen pancreatic inflammation and function.

Pancreatic Insufficiency.message: These drugs can reduce effectiveness of pancreatic enzyme replacement.

Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia.message: These drugs can worsen urinary obstruction and retention.

Urinary Retention.message: These drugs can worsen or precipitate acute urinary retention.

Urinary Incontinence.message: These drugs can worsen incontinence symptoms.

Overactive Bladder.message: These drugs can worsen urgency and frequency symptoms.

Interstitial Cystitis.message: These can trigger bladder pain and worsen symptoms.

Urinary Tract Infection Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent and complicated UTIs.

Nephrolithiasis.message: These drugs can promote kidney stone formation.

Chronic Pelvic Pain.message: Long-term NSAID use requires monitoring for adverse effects.

Endometriosis.message: Estrogen can stimulate endometrial tissue growth and worsen symptoms.

Polycystic Ovary Syndrome.message: These drugs require monitoring of metabolic parameters.

Menopause.message: Hormone therapy requires careful assessment of risks and benefits.

Osteopenia.message: These drugs can accelerate bone loss and increase fracture risk.

Vitamin D Deficiency.message: Vitamin D should be repleted before starting bisphosphonate therapy.

Hypercalcemia.message: These drugs can worsen hypercalcemia and related complications.

Hypocalcemia.message: These drugs can worsen hypocalcemia and require monitoring.

Hypokalemia.message: These drugs can worsen potassium depletion and cardiac arrhythmias.

Hyperkalemia.message: These drugs can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia.

Hyponatremia.message: These drugs can worsen hyponatremia and neurological symptoms.

Hypernatremia.message: These can worsen hypernatremia and require monitoring.

Hypomagnesemia.message: These drugs can cause or worsen magnesium depletion.

Metabolic Acidosis.message: These drugs can worsen acidosis and require monitoring.

Metabolic Alkalosis.message: These drugs can worsen alkalosis and electrolyte imbalances.

Dehydration.message: These drugs can worsen dehydration and cause complications.

Syndrome of Inappropriate Antidiuretic H.message: These drugs can worsen SIADH and hyponatremia.

Diabetes Insipidus.message: These drugs can cause or worsen diabetes insipidus.

Erectile Dysfunction.message: These drugs can worsen erectile dysfunction.

Priapism History.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent priapism.

Testicular Torsion History.message: Some antipsychotics may increase torsion risk through hormonal effects.

Gynecomastia.message: These drugs can cause or worsen gynecomastia.

Hirsutism.message: These drugs can worsen unwanted hair growth.

Alopecia.message: These drugs can cause or worsen hair loss.

Psoriasis.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen psoriatic flares.

Eczema.message: Long-term topical steroids require monitoring for skin atrophy.

Rosacea.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen rosacea flares.

Photosensitivity.message: These drugs increase risk of severe sunburn and skin reactions.

Vitiligo.message: These should be used cautiously to avoid worsening depigmentation.

Melasma.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen melasma.

Acne.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen acne.

Stevens-Johnson Syndrome History.message: These drugs have highest risk of causing recurrent severe skin reactions.

Angioedema History.message: These drugs can cause recurrent life-threatening angioedema.

Urticaria.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen hives.

Contact Dermatitis.message: These can cause allergic contact dermatitis in sensitized patients.

Wound Healing Impairment.message: These drugs can delay wound healing and increase infection risk.

Diabetic Foot.message: These drugs can worsen peripheral perfusion and delay healing.

Pressure Ulcer.message: These drugs can delay healing and increase infection risk.

Cellulitis Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent and severe skin infections.

Fungal Infection Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent and invasive fungal infections.

Herpes Zoster History.message: These drugs increase risk of shingles reactivation and complications.

Cold Sore Recurrent.message: These can trigger herpes simplex reactivation.

Warts.message: These drugs can worsen viral warts and increase spread.

Tinea Pedis.message: Steroids alone can worsen fungal infections (tinea incognito).

Onychomycosis.message: These require monitoring for drug interactions and hepatotoxicity.

Scabies.message: Steroids can worsen scabies infestation (crusted scabies).

Lice Infestation.message: These require proper use to avoid toxicity, especially in children.

Bedbugs.message: Treatment focuses on environmental control, not systemic medications.

Peripheral Neuropathy.message: These drugs can worsen nerve damage and neuropathic symptoms.

Diabetic Neuropathy.message: These drugs can worsen diabetic nerve damage.

Guillain-Barre Syndrome History.message: These may trigger recurrence in susceptible patients.

Bell Palsy.message: These drugs can worsen dry eye and oral complications.

Trigeminal Neuralgia.message: These drugs may trigger facial pain episodes.

Restless Legs Syndrome.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen restless legs symptoms.

Tardive Dyskinesia.message: Continued antipsychotic use can worsen irreversible movement disorder.

Essential Tremor.message: These drugs can worsen tremor amplitude.

Huntington Disease.message: These drugs can worsen chorea and psychiatric symptoms.

Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis.message: These drugs can worsen respiratory function in ALS patients.

Muscular Dystrophy.message: These drugs can worsen muscle weakness and damage.

Rhabdomyolysis History.message: These drugs significantly increase risk of recurrent rhabdomyolysis.

Fibromyalgia.message: Opioids are not recommended for fibromyalgia and may worsen symptoms.

Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.message: These drugs can worsen fatigue and cognitive symptoms.

Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrom.message: These drugs can worsen orthostatic symptoms.

Syncope Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent fainting episodes.

Vertigo.message: These drugs can worsen balance and vestibular symptoms.

Meniere Disease.message: These can trigger vertigo attacks and worsen symptoms.

Tinnitus.message: These drugs can cause or worsen tinnitus.

Hearing Loss.message: These drugs can cause irreversible hearing damage.

Otitis Media Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent and complicated ear infections.

Otosclerosis.message: Effect on otosclerosis is uncertain; requires monitoring.

Epistaxis Recurrent.message: These drugs increase risk of recurrent nosebleeds.

Sinusitis Chronic.message: These require monitoring for fungal infections and complications.

Nasal Polyps.message: Aspirin can trigger severe reactions in aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease.

Allergic Rhinitis.message: These can cause excessive sedation and anticholinergic effects.

Dry Eye Syndrome.message: These drugs can worsen dry eye symptoms.

Cataract.message: Long-term steroid use can accelerate cataract formation.

Macular Degeneration.message: Some supplements may worsen macular degeneration.

Retinopathy.message: These drugs require regular ophthalmologic monitoring.

Optic Neuritis.message: These drugs can cause optic nerve damage and vision loss.

Uveitis.message: Sudden withdrawal can trigger severe inflammatory flares.

Keratitis.message: Steroids can worsen infectious keratitis and delay healing.

Conjunctivitis Recurrent.message: These can perpetuate infection or allergic inflammation.

Strabismus.message: These drugs can affect eye muscle coordination.

Diplopia.message: These drugs can worsen double vision.

Nystagmus.message: These drugs can cause or worsen involuntary eye movements.

Oral Candidiasis.message: These drugs increase risk of oral fungal infections.

Periodontitis.message: These can worsen gum disease and cause gingival hyperplasia.

Xerostomia.message: These drugs can worsen dry mouth and increase dental complications.

Geographic Tongue.message: Certain medications and foods can trigger painful episodes.

Burning Mouth Syndrome.message: Some drugs can cause or worsen burning mouth symptoms.

Oral Lichen Planus.message: These drugs can trigger lichenoid reactions.

Aphthous Ulcers Recurrent.message: These drugs can cause or worsen mouth ulcers.

Temporomandibular Joint Disorder.message: These drugs increase risk of osteonecrosis of the jaw with dental procedures.

Bruxism.message: These drugs can worsen teeth grinding and jaw clenching.

Dental Abscess.message: These drugs increase risk of severe and spreading dental infections.

Cleft Lip/Palate.message: These drugs increase risk of orofacial malformations.

Laryngitis.message: These drugs can cause or worsen voice changes and cough.

Vocal Cord Dysfunction.message: These drugs can trigger vocal cord spasm episodes.

Dyspnea.message: These drugs can worsen breathing difficulties.

Orthopnea.message: These require careful titration to avoid worsening symptoms.

Pleural Effusion.message: These drugs can cause drug-induced pleural effusion.

Pneumothorax History.message: These procedures increase risk of recurrent pneumothorax.

Sarcoidosis.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen sarcoid flares.

Silicosis.message: These can worsen pulmonary symptoms and function.

Asbestosis.message: These increase risk of malignancy and respiratory complications.

Pneumoconiosis.message: These can worsen occupational lung disease.

Latex Allergy.message: These can cause severe allergic reactions including anaphylaxis.

Food Allergy.message: Hidden food allergens in medications can trigger reactions.

Anaphylaxis History.message: These require premedication and emergency preparedness.

Mastocytosis.message: These drugs can trigger severe systemic reactions.

Hereditary Angioedema.message: These drugs can trigger life-threatening angioedema attacks.

Chronic Urticaria.message: These drugs can trigger or worsen chronic hives.

Eosinophilic Esophagitis.message: Dietary triggers can worsen esophageal inflammation.

Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis.message: Continued exposure can cause progressive lung damage.

Contact Allergy.message: These can cause severe contact dermatitis in sensitized patients.

Drug Allergy.message: Structurally similar drugs may cause cross-reactions.

Vaccine Allergy.message: Alternative vaccines or special protocols may be needed.

Insect Sting Allergy.message: Beta-blockers can interfere with epinephrine treatment and worsen reactions.

Radiation-Induced Mucositis.message: Harsh products can worsen radiation-damaged mucosa.

Post-Operative Ileus.message: These drugs can prolong ileus and delay recovery.

Post-Operative Nausea.message: These drugs increase PONV risk and require prophylaxis.

Post-Operative Delirium.message: These drugs increase delirium risk in elderly surgical patients.

Pseudocholinesterase Deficiency.message: These drugs cause prolonged paralysis in enzyme-deficient patients.

Porphyria.message: These drugs can precipitate life-threatening acute attacks.

Hemochromatosis.message: These can worsen iron overload and organ damage.

Wilson Disease.message: Additional copper can worsen neurological and hepatic damage.

Marfan Syndrome.message: These drugs may increase risk of aortic dissection.

Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome.message: These significantly increase bleeding risk in vascular EDS.

Osteogenesis Imperfecta.message: These drugs increase fracture risk in brittle bone disease.

Achondroplasia.message: These require special considerations due to spinal anatomy.

Down Syndrome.message: Atlantoaxial instability increases risk of spinal cord injury.

Fragile X Syndrome.message: These may worsen anxiety and behavioral symptoms.

Phenylketonuria.message: These contain phenylalanine and can cause neurological damage.

Galactosemia.message: Galactose sources must be strictly avoided.

Homocystinuria.message: These can worsen metabolic imbalance and complications.

Fabry Disease.message: These can worsen renal complications of Fabry disease.

Gaucher Disease.message: These can worsen cytopenias in Gaucher patients.

Tay-Sachs Disease.message: These can worsen aspiration risk and respiratory complications.

Mucopolysaccharidosis.message: Difficult airway requires special anesthetic planning.

Glycogen Storage Disease.message: These can trigger hypoglycemia and metabolic crisis.

Mitochondrial Disease.message: These drugs can worsen mitochondrial dysfunction.

Lesch-Nyhan Syndrome.message: Special considerations needed for hyperuricemia management.

Cri-du-Chat Syndrome.message: These increase aspiration risk due to anatomical abnormalities.

Prader-Willi Syndrome.message: These can worsen hyperphagia and obesity complications.

Angelman Syndrome.message: These can worsen ataxia and seizure control.

Rett Syndrome.message: These increase risk of sudden cardiac death.

Williams Syndrome.message: These can worsen hypercalcemia in Williams syndrome.

Turner Syndrome.message: These require cardiac evaluation before hormone therapy.

Klinefelter Syndrome.message: These require monitoring for metabolic effects and complications.

Neurofibromatosis.message: These may require special precautions for imaging.

Tuberous Sclerosis.message: These may worsen tumor growth in TSC patients.
